scope.summary:
Goal: Add a per-document reading/preview mode to the native rich-text editor so any note can be flipped between an editable WYSIWYG view and a clean, read-only rendered presentation. This is the Obsidian 'reading view' parity item and the native equivalent of the React ViewModeToggle: the GUI look-and-behavior doc merges the toggle into the native editor chrome rather than exposing a separate floating control. The chosen mode is persisted per document_id so reopening a note restores its last view. This MT does NOT introduce a new rendering path — it reuses the MT-011 DocModel as the single source of truth and the MT-012 renderer pipeline, switching only the interaction/chrome behavior between Edit and Reading.

Expected diff shape: (a) one new Rust module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/reading_mode.rs (~180–300 lines) defining the ViewMode enum, a per-document mode store keyed on document_id, and the toggle widget; (b) edits to the existing renderer widget at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs to honor a read_only flag (suppress caret, suppress editable TextEdit runs, apply reading margins/typography, keep links and embeds interactive); (c) a one-line registration in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/mod.rs (pub mod reading_mode;) if the module is not already declared; (d) kittest + cargo unit tests under src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_*.rs (flat test files, e.g. src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_reading_mode.rs).

Files to CREATE:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/reading_mode.rs — defines:
    * `pub enum ViewMode { Edit, Reading }` deriving Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize.
    * `pub struct ReadingModeStore { modes: std::collections::HashMap<String, ViewMode> }` keyed on document_id (String). Methods: `get(&self, document_id: &str) -> ViewMode` (default ViewMode::Edit when absent), `set(&mut self, document_id: &str, mode: ViewMode)`, `toggle(&mut self, document_id: &str) -> ViewMode`.
    * `pub fn reading_mode_store(ctx: &egui::Context) -> ReadingModeStore` / a persisted accessor: store the ReadingModeStore in egui Context data via `ctx.data_mut(|d| d.get_persisted_mut_or_default::<ReadingModeStore>(egui::Id::new("handshake_rich_reading_modes")))` so the per-document choice survives re-render and (via egui persistence) app restart. ReadingModeStore must derive Clone, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize so egui's persistence layer can round-trip it.
    * `pub fn view_mode_toggle(ui: &mut egui::Ui, document_id: &str, store: &mut ReadingModeStore) -> ViewMode` — renders an Edit|Reading segmented toggle inside the native editor chrome and returns the active mode after handling clicks. AccessKit: the toggle container author_id="rich-reading-mode-toggle" role=Group; the Edit segment author_id="rich-reading-mode-edit" role=Button (or ToggleButton) actions=[Press]; the Reading segment author_id="rich-reading-mode-reading" role=Button actions=[Press]; mark the active segment selected/toggled in the AccessKit node so swarm agents and screen readers can read current mode. Use the existing WP-011 accessibility/* helpers (accessibility/mod.rs, accessibility/registry.rs) to register author_ids and toggled state rather than emitting AccessKit nodes inline.

Files to EDIT:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs — the MT-012 WYSIWYG widget. Add a `read_only: bool` input (or accept a ViewMode) on the show/render entry point. When read_only is true: (1) do NOT instantiate the editable caret/selection state and do NOT emit egui TextEdit widgets for runs — render runs as immutable galleys/labels via the MT-012 block renderer in a non-editable code path; (2) widen the content margins to reading width (e.g. clamp the text column to ~700–760 logical px centered, larger vertical paragraph spacing) using the existing WP-011 theme tokens (theme/mod.rs, theme/palette.rs), not hardcoded hex; (3) keep wikilink chips (MT-015) and embeds/node-views (MT-014) interactive — their click/hover handlers must still fire and route through the shared navigation/command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); (4) skip input-handler edit dispatch (typing, formatting shortcuts) so the document cannot be mutated while in Reading mode. When read_only is false, behavior is exactly the MT-012 editable path (no regression).
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/mod.rs — add `pub mod reading_mode;` if absent.
- src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml — only if serde derive on ViewMode/ReadingModeStore requires enabling the serde feature; reuse the workspace serde already used by MT-011 DocModel, do not add a new dependency if serde is already present.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Define ViewMode + ReadingModeStore in reading_mode.rs. Keep the store pure data; the egui-context accessor lives in the same module but the store type itself must be testable without egui (so the persistence unit test can construct a ReadingModeStore directly).
2. In the rich editor host (the same call site that already builds the MT-012 widget), read the active mode: `let mode = store.get(document_id);` then render the view_mode_toggle in the chrome and pass `read_only = mode == ViewMode::Reading` into rich_editor_widget.
3. In rich_editor_widget.rs, branch on read_only at the top of the per-block render loop. Reuse the MT-012 block renderer for the actual glyph/galley layout — do NOT fork a second renderer; the only difference between Edit and Reading is: caret/selection state allocation, TextEdit emission, input dispatch, and margin/typography. This keeps the DocModel as the single source of truth (MT-011) and guarantees Reading shows exactly what Edit shows, minus editing affordances.
4. Caret suppression: in Reading mode, do not allocate the caret blink state and do not call the input handler that advances caret position; the AccessKit tree for the rendered region must NOT contain an editable TextField/TextInput node for the document body (the test asserts this).
5. Interactivity preservation: wikilink chips and embeds register their own egui responses; ensure the Reading code path still calls their response builders so clicks route to the shared navigation/command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs). Do not gate link/embed click handling behind the editable branch.
6. Persistence: every toggle calls store.set(document_id, new_mode) and writes back to ctx.data_mut so the choice is keyed per document_id and survives re-render; egui's persisted storage carries it across app restart. On render, always read store.get(document_id) so switching documents shows each document's own remembered mode.
7. Tests (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_*.rs flat files, e.g. tests/test_reading_mode.rs):
   - cargo unit test: construct ReadingModeStore, toggle for document_id "doc-A", assert returned mode flips Edit->Reading->Edit; assert get("doc-B") still defaults to Edit (per-document isolation).
   - cargo unit test: toggling to Reading yields read_only=true; assert the widget input the host passes (read_only flag) is true and that no caret/selection state is constructed in that path (e.g. via a test-visible flag or by asserting the render config).
   - kittest harness: seed a DocModel with a heading, a bullet list, a quote, and a wikilink; render in Reading mode; dump the AccessKit tree and assert the heading/list/quote nodes are present AND that no editable TextEdit/TextInput node exists for the document body.
   - kittest screenshot: capture mt055_reading_mode.png of the rendered read-only document and assert it renders (non-empty, headings/list visible) — store under the WP test_output convention.
   - cargo test: persistence across re-render — set mode=Reading for document_id, simulate a second render frame reading from the same store, assert mode is still Reading (choice persists per document_id across re-render).

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
- MT-011 (BlockDocumentModel / DocModel) must be merged — reading mode renders the same DocModel.
- MT-012 (WYSIWYG rendering) must be merged — reading mode reuses its block renderer in a read-only branch.
- MT-013 (formatting blocks) must be merged — headings/lists/quotes/formatting must already exist so reading view can render them.
- MT-014 (embeds/node-views) and MT-015 (wikilinks) are not hard dependencies for this MT to compile, but the interop edge requires their click handlers remain wired in the read-only path when they are present; guard the calls so MT-055 does not break if a build predates them.
- WP-011 dependency (cross-WP, prose only): reuses existing WP-011 modules in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ — accessibility/* (author_id registration + toggled state), theme/* (reading typography tokens), and the command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs pair as the shared navigation/command bus. These are already merged from WP-011 and are not WP-012 microtask dependencies.

Backend: NONE. This MT is pure frontend view-state; it binds no handshake_core API. Mode persistence uses egui's local context persistence, not the EventLedger. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only applies to backend state and is not touched here.

Risk if missed: Obsidian core 'reading view' is absent; long notes have no distraction-free rendered presentation and the parity proof suite (MT-044) cannot assert Edit|Reading parity.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: Toggling to Reading sets read_only=true and suppresses caret state — cargo test proves that the Reading branch passes read_only=true into rich_editor_widget and constructs no caret/selection state.

AC-002: Reading mode re-renders the same MT-011 DocModel as a clean read-only view — the rendered output for a seeded DocModel (heading, bullet list, quote) matches the Edit-mode content minus editing affordances; proven by kittest AccessKit tree containing the heading/list/quote nodes.
AC-003: No editable TextEdit/TextInput node for the document body exists in the AccessKit tree while in Reading mode — proven by kittest AccessKit tree dump.

AC-004: Wikilink chips (MT-015) and embeds (MT-014) remain clickable in Reading mode and route through the shared navigation/command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) — proven by kittest interaction test firing a wikilink click in Reading mode and observing the navigation event.

AC-005: Reading mode applies larger reading margins/typography (centered content column, wider paragraph spacing) using WP-011 theme tokens (theme/*), not hardcoded colors/dimensions — proven by screenshot mt055_reading_mode.png and code inspection.

AC-006: The Edit|Reading toggle renders in the native editor chrome with AccessKit author_ids 'rich-reading-mode-toggle' (Group), 'rich-reading-mode-edit' (Button), 'rich-reading-mode-reading' (Button) registered via accessibility/*, and marks the active segment selected — proven by AccessKit tree dump.

AC-007: Mode choice persists per document_id across re-render — cargo test sets Reading for one document_id, reads the store on a second frame, asserts Reading; and asserts a different document_id still defaults to Edit (per-document isolation).

AC-008: Switching back to Edit returns the exact MT-012 WYSIWYG editor with no regression (caret, selection, typing, formatting all work) — proven by kittest that toggles Reading then Edit and edits a run successfully.
AC-009: cargo test -p handshake-native across src/frontend/handshake_native/tests passes with no failures for the MT-055 reading-mode tests.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native reading_mode -- --nocapture => toggle/persistence unit tests GREEN, no panics
PT-002: kittest: render seeded DocModel in Reading mode, dump AccessKit tree, assert heading/list/quote present AND no document-body TextEdit/TextInput node
PT-003: kittest screenshot mt055_reading_mode.png saved under WP test_output, non-empty, headings/list visible
PT-004: kittest: click a wikilink chip in Reading mode, assert navigation event routes through the shared navigation/command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs)
PT-005: cargo test -p handshake-native: set Reading for document_id, re-render, assert mode still Reading; assert second document_id defaults to Edit
implementation_notes: Reuse the MT-012 block renderer for Reading mode; do NOT fork a second renderer — the ONLY differences between Edit and Reading are caret/selection allocation, TextEdit emission, input dispatch, and margin/typography. This keeps the MT-011 DocModel as the single source of truth.

ViewMode and ReadingModeStore derive serde::Serialize/Deserialize and Default so egui's get_persisted_mut_or_default round-trips the per-document map across app restart; keep ReadingModeStore egui-free so it is unit-testable without a Context.
Use egui::Id::new("handshake_rich_reading_modes") as the stable persisted key; the host reads store.get(document_id) every frame so each document shows its own remembered mode.

Reading typography: clamp the content column to ~700-760 logical px centered and widen paragraph spacing via WP-011 theme tokens (theme/mod.rs, theme/palette.rs — visuals/spacing), never hardcoded hex or px literals scattered inline.
Guard MT-014/MT-015 click handlers so they still fire in the read-only branch when present, but the build does not break on a tree that predates those MTs (feature/None guard).

AccessKit: mark the active toggle segment as selected/toggled (set the toggled state on the node) via the WP-011 accessibility/* helpers (accessibility/mod.rs, accessibility/registry.rs) so a no-context swarm agent can read the current view mode without inferring from labels.

Cross-WP reuse (WP-011, prose dependency — not a WP-012 microtask depends_on): the shared navigation/command bus is the existing command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs pair; reading typography uses theme/*; AccessKit author_id registration uses accessibility/*. These WP-011 modules already exist under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ and must be reused, not re-created.
Persistence here is egui local context persistence only — do NOT route view-mode state through the EventLedger/PostgreSQL; this is pure frontend view-state with no backend binding.

REUSE THE MT-012 RENDERER - NO 2ND RENDERER (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24 - the Edit|Reading PARITY INVARIANT, RISK-001/MC-001): Reading mode reuses the EXISTING MT-012 block renderer in rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs via a read_only branch - the ONLY differences are (a) no caret/selection state allocation, (b) no egui TextEdit emission (render runs as immutable galleys/labels), (c) no input-handler edit dispatch, (d) reading margins/typography. Do NOT fork a 2nd renderer (a forked renderer drifts -> Reading shows different output than Edit, breaking the MT-044 parity proof). MT-011 DocModel stays the SINGLE source of truth. The paths are correct (rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs is the real MT-011/012 widget); allowed_paths tightened to tests/test_reading_mode.rs + theme/palette.rs (for the reading-typography tokens).

NO-EDIT IN READING + LINKS/EMBEDS LIVE + PERSISTENCE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): (RISK-002/MC-002, AC-003) the Reading AccessKit tree MUST contain ZERO editable TextEdit/TextInput node for the document body (hard test assertion). (RISK-005/MC-005, AC-001) no caret/selection alloc + no input dispatch in Reading -> editing-attempt leaves the DocModel UNCHANGED (test edits in Reading + asserts DocModel unchanged). (RISK-003/MC-003, AC-004) wikilink chips (MT-015) + embeds (MT-014) STAY interactive in read-only - their click handlers fire + route through the shared bus (command_registry+event_bus); MT-014/015 EXIST now (E2 base done) so wire them directly (the predates-guard is moot but harmless). (RISK-004/MC-004, AC-007) mode persists PER document_id via ctx.data_mut get_persisted_mut_or_default::<ReadingModeStore>(egui::Id::new('handshake_rich_reading_modes')) - survives re-render; ReadingModeStore is egui-FREE + serde+Default so it round-trips + is unit-testable without a Context; per-document isolation (doc-B still defaults Edit). NOT EventLedger/Postgres - pure frontend view-state (binds NONE).

THEME TYPOGRAPHY + CHROME + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): reading margins/typography (centered content column ~700-760 logical px, wider paragraph spacing) - the COLUMN-WIDTH/spacing may be named consts, but any COLOR goes through theme/palette.rs tokens (RISK-006/MC-006 - NO Color32::from_rgb literal, the CONTROL-4 guard; readable in light+dark). The Edit|Reading segmented toggle (rich-reading-mode-toggle Group / rich-reading-mode-edit Button / rich-reading-mode-reading Button via accessibility/registry.rs, active segment marked toggled/selected) renders at the WIDGET level (provable now in kittest); its full editor-CHROME placement integrates at E11 host-mount (like the menu entries - MT-050/052/053 precedent). Switching back to Edit = the EXACT MT-012 editable path, no regression (AC-008 - toggle Reading then Edit + edit a run successfully). Screenshot to the EXTERNAL wp-kernel-012-mt-055 root (mt055_reading_mode.png; NOT repo-local test_output/ - CX-212E; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty). PURE frontend (no live PG); full suite via -j 2 (~90 bins OOM); clippy clean; no unwrap.
ports_from_react: app/src/App.tsx — ViewMode state (Edit vs Reading per-document model)
app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx — read vs edit rendering branch; the read-only rendering path to port into src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs
app/src/components ViewModeToggle — Edit|Reading segmented toggle UI; native equivalent merged into the editor chrome per the GUI look-and-behavior doc
binds_backend_api: NONE — pure frontend view-state; no handshake_core API bound. Per-document mode persists via egui local context persistence, not the EventLedger/PostgreSQL.

risks: RISK-001: Reading mode forks a second renderer that drifts from the MT-012 editable renderer, so the read-only view shows different output than Edit mode — breaking the Edit|Reading parity invariant the MT-044 proof suite asserts.
RISK-002: A latent editable TextEdit/TextInput node remains in the AccessKit tree in Reading mode, allowing a swarm agent or screen reader to edit a document that is supposed to be read-only.

RISK-003: Wikilink chips (MT-015) and embeds (MT-014) become non-interactive in Reading mode because link/embed click handling was gated behind the editable branch, severing the shared navigation/command bus from read-only documents.
RISK-004: Mode persistence keyed incorrectly (global instead of per document_id) so switching documents leaks one document's Reading state onto another, or the choice is lost on re-render/restart.
RISK-005: Reading mode mutates the DocModel (e.g. caret/input handler still active) so opening a note for reading silently changes it.
RISK-006: Reading typography uses hardcoded px/hex instead of theme tokens, breaking light/dark theming and producing unreadable contrast in one theme.
RISK-007: Build breaks when MT-014/MT-015 are not yet merged because the read-only path calls their handlers unconditionally.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Single-renderer enforcement — reading mode MUST reuse the MT-012 block renderer; a test asserts the same DocModel produces matching block structure (headings/list/quote nodes) in both modes.
MC-002: AccessKit tree dump in Reading mode MUST contain zero editable TextEdit/TextInput nodes for the document body; gated as a hard test assertion.
MC-003: A kittest fires a wikilink click in Reading mode and asserts the navigation event reaches the shared navigation/command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); embeds remain clickable.
MC-004: Persistence test asserts mode is keyed per document_id (one document Reading, a second document defaults Edit) and survives a second render frame.
MC-005: Read-only branch must not allocate caret/selection state or dispatch input edits; a test edits in Reading mode and asserts the DocModel is unchanged.
MC-006: Reading typography/margins must use WP-011 theme tokens (theme/*); code inspection plus a screenshot in both themes (or at least the default theme) confirms readable contrast.
MC-007: MT-014/MT-015 handler calls in the read-only path are guarded so the crate compiles and tests pass on a build that predates those MTs.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs (MT-011/012) + MT-014 embeds + MT-015 wikilinks + theme tokens + egui persistence + MT-050/052/053 chrome-at-E11
corrections: reuse MT-012 block renderer (read_only branch) - NO 2nd renderer (Edit|Reading parity invariant); DocModel single source
Reading AccessKit has NO editable node + no caret/input (DocModel unchanged on edit-attempt); links/embeds stay interactive (MT-014/015 exist); per-document egui persistence (not EventLedger)
reading typography via named consts + theme COLOR tokens (no Color32 literal); toggle provable at widget level, chrome placement at E11
external screenshot (mt055_reading_mode.png not test_output/); pure frontend (binds NONE); -j 2 full suite; Edit return = exact MT-012 no-regress

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_9ae5c67f (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: 57400f1a feat MT-055 per-document reading mode (Obsidian reading view)
cab1ebae fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..009 + PT-001..005 PASS; 5 unit + 9 integration + regression (rich_editor_widget 6, formatting 14, find_replace 7, e7 13, ckc_embed 11, theme 19, accesskit 9) green; clippy clean; reading view (H1 'Reading View Title' + bullet + green-accent blockquote + interactive 'Target Note' wikilink, centered column) visually verified - Obsidian reading-view parity; screenshot EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.steers_held: reuse the MT-012 renderer: render_blocks is the SINGLE function for both modes (read_only only sets caret_block=None + centered column + extra gap; paint_block byte-for-byte same) - NO 2nd renderer (the Edit|Reading parity invariant); ZERO editable body TextInput node in Reading AccessKit (pt002); NO DocModel mutation (typed X+Enter -> content_json byte-identical); wikilinks/embeds STAY interactive (chip click -> WikilinkActivated, NOT gated behind editable branch); per-document egui persistence (doc-A Reading persists, doc-B defaults Edit); reading typography via named consts + theme COLOR tokens (no Color32 literal, test_theme green); toggle accesskit ids+toggled; Edit-return = exact MT-012 no-regression.
implementation_result.honest_note: didn't produce a side-by-side Edit screenshot for the strict parity comparison (weakest unproven point, mitigated by the shared-renderer being literally one function render_blocks). Toggle proven at widget level; full editor-chrome placement at E11.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: rich_editor/reading_mode.rs (new) + rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs (read_only branch) + rich_editor/mod.rs + test in allowed_paths (theme/palette.rs + Cargo.toml not needed - existing tokens/serde reused).
